enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source password manager for teams with client-side encryption, free for up to 10 users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Psono is an open-source, self-hosted password manager with client-side encryption, free for teams of up to 10 users.

features.title: Client-side encryption
features.description: Credentials are encrypted client-side before transport and storage, using multiple layers of encryption.

features.title: Self-hosted deployment
features.description: Deploy Psono on your own servers for full control over where credential data is stored.

features.title: SaaS option
features.description: Psono is also available as a hosted SaaS edition for teams that don't want to manage their own infrastructure.

features.title: Open-source codebase
features.description: The source code is publicly available, which the company says allows for independent security audits.

features.title: Encrypted credential sharing
features.description: Teams can securely share passwords and credentials with other members through encrypted sharing.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Apps are available for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus mobile apps for iOS and Android, and browser extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge.

pros: Open-source code allows for independent security audits
Free plan includes all business features for up to 10 users
Self-hosted option gives full control over data residency
Available across desktop, mobile, and browser platforms
Rated 4.7/5 on G2 and 4.5/5 on the Chrome Web Store
cons: Pricing for teams larger than 10 users is not published and requires contacting the company
Self-hosting requires server management expertise
No published details on enterprise SLA pricing

faqs.question: Is Psono free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes. Psono offers all business features free for up to 10 users.

faqs.question: Can Psono be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes. Psono can be deployed on-premise on your own servers, or used as a hosted SaaS edition.

faqs.question: Is Psono open source?
faqs.answer: Yes. Psono's source code is publicly available, which the company says allows for independent security audits.

faqs.question: What platforms does Psono support?
faqs.answer: Psono has apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux, mobile apps for iOS and Android, and extensions for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge.

use_cases.title: Team credential sharing
use_cases.description: Teams can store and securely share business credentials with encrypted sharing between members.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted password management
use_cases.description: Organizations that need to keep credential data on their own infrastructure can self-host Psono on-premise.
use_cases.audience: IT and security teams with compliance requirements

features: Professional support and SLAs available on request

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Psono?
paragraphs: Psono is an open-source password manager built for businesses and teams, encrypting credentials client-side before they are transmitted or stored.
It can be deployed on a company's own servers as a self-hosted, on-premise solution, or used as a hosted SaaS edition, giving organizations flexibility over where their data lives.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Psono is for
paragraphs: Psono is aimed at teams and businesses that want a shared, encrypted vault for company credentials, with the option to self-host for full data control. Its free tier covers teams of up to 10 users with all business features included.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source, end-to-end encrypted password manager with notes, file storage, and a built-in authenticator.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Padloc is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted password manager with a free plan and paid tiers from $3.49/month.

features.title: Unlimited vault items and devices
features.description: All tiers, including the free plan, allow unlimited vault items and unlimited connected devices.

features.title: Built-in authenticator
features.description: Padloc includes a built-in authenticator for generating one-time passwords, alongside password storage.

features.title: Encrypted file storage
features.description: Store documents and files in the encrypted vault, with storage limits varying by plan.

features.title: Shared vaults
features.description: Family, Team, and Business plans support shared vaults for collaborative access to credentials.

features.title: Security reporting
features.description: Padloc includes security reporting capabilities to help flag weak or reused passwords.

features.title: Markdown notes
features.description: Store rich-text notes with markdown support alongside passwords and other vault items.

pros: Audited by three different, independent groups of security experts
Open-source codebase with publicly available source
Free plan available, plus a 30-day free trial on paid plans
Unlimited vault items and devices across all tiers
Works across Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, and major browsers
cons: Individual plans (Premium, Team) are priced separately from Family and Business plans, which can be confusing
Enterprise pricing is custom quote-only
File storage limits vary by tier and are not detailed in a single comparison

faqs.question: Is there a free version of Padloc?
faqs.answer: Yes, Padloc offers a free plan at $0/month with basic features.

faqs.question: Does Padloc offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include a 30-day free trial.

faqs.question: Has Padloc been security audited?
faqs.answer: Yes, Padloc states it has been audited by three different, independent groups of security experts.

faqs.question: What platforms does Padloc support?
faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, iOS, Android, and major browsers via Chrome and Firefox extensions.

use_cases.title: Personal password management
use_cases.description: Individuals can use the Premium plan for unlimited encrypted password and file storage.

use_cases.title: Family password sharing
use_cases.description: The Family plan lets households share vaults for passwords and sensitive documents.

use_cases.title: Team credential management
use_cases.description: Team and Business plans provide shared vaults for small businesses to manage company credentials.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Padloc?
paragraphs: Padloc is an open-source, end-to-end encrypted platform for storing passwords, notes, documents, and other sensitive information.
It includes a built-in authenticator for one-time passwords, and the company says its code has been audited by three independent groups of security experts.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Padloc is for
paragraphs: Padloc serves individuals, families, and teams that want an open-source, audited password manager with a free tier and paid plans that add shared vaults, file storage, and security reporting.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source, cross-platform password manager compatible with KeePass database files.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: KeeWeb is a free, open-source password manager compatible with KeePass, with desktop apps and cloud storage sync.

features.title: KeePass compatibility
features.description: KeeWeb works with existing KeePass password database files.

features.title: Multi-file support
features.description: Open multiple password database files at once with unified search across all of them.

features.title: Password generator
features.description: Generate new passwords with customizable parameters.

features.title: Protected fields
features.description: Sensitive fields are hidden by default and only revealed on demand.

features.title: Entry history with rollback
features.description: Previous versions of an entry are tracked and can be restored.

features.title: Regex search
features.description: Advanced search supports regular expressions for finding entries.

features.title: Cloud storage integrations
features.description: Sync database files with Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or a custom WebDAV server.

pros: Completely free with no trials, demos, or limits, per the website
Open source, with source code available on GitHub
Compatible with existing KeePass password database files
Syncs with Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or custom WebDAV servers
Available as a desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus a browser-based web version
cons: No dedicated official iOS or Android app mentioned on the site
No built-in team sharing or collaboration features
No official paid support option

faqs.question: Is KeeWeb free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the website states the app is completely free, with no trials, demo versions, or limits.

faqs.question: Is KeeWeb compatible with KeePass?
faqs.answer: Yes, KeeWeb is compatible with KeePass password database files.

faqs.question: What cloud storage services does KeeWeb support?
faqs.answer: Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, and custom WebDAV servers.

faqs.question: What platforms does KeeWeb support?
faqs.answer: Desktop apps for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus a web version that runs in modern browsers.

use_cases.title: Personal password management
use_cases.description: Individuals already using KeePass can manage their existing password databases with KeeWeb's interface.

use_cases.title: Cross-device syncing
use_cases.description: Sync a password database across devices using Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or WebDAV.
use_cases.audience: Users who want cloud-synced password access

long_form_blocks.heading: What is KeeWeb?
paragraphs: KeeWeb is a free, cross-platform password manager compatible with KeePass database files.
It's available as a desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux, and as a web app that runs in modern browsers, with the source code available on GitHub.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who KeeWeb is for
paragraphs: KeeWeb is aimed at people who already use, or want to use, KeePass-format password databases and want a free client with cloud storage sync via Dropbox, Google Drive, OneDrive, or WebDAV.

enrichment.short_description: Free, open-source Rust server that implements the Bitwarden Client API for self-hosted password management.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Vaultwarden is a free, open-source, self-hosted server compatible with official Bitwarden password manager clients.

features.title: Bitwarden API compatibility
features.description: Implements the Bitwarden Client API in Rust, so official Bitwarden mobile, desktop, and browser clients can connect to it.

features.title: Personal vault management
features.description: Store and manage personal passwords and vault items.

features.title: Send
features.description: Share text or files securely with the built-in Send feature.

features.title: Organizations
features.description: Supports organizations with collections, password sharing, member roles, and groups.

features.title: Multi-factor authentication
features.description: Supports Authenticator apps, Email, FIDO2 WebAuthn, YubiKey, and Duo for MFA.

features.title: Emergency access
features.description: Grant trusted contacts emergency access to a vault.

features.title: Admin backend
features.description: Includes an admin backend interface for managing the self-hosted instance.

pros: Free and open source under the AGPL-3.0 license
Compatible with official Bitwarden client apps across mobile, desktop, and browser
Written in Rust using the Rocket framework, described as lightweight for self-hosting
Supports strong MFA options including FIDO2 WebAuthn and YubiKey
Active community project with 63.8k GitHub stars
cons: Unofficial project, not affiliated with or officially supported by Bitwarden Inc.
Requires self-hosting and container knowledge (Docker/Podman) to deploy
Requires HTTPS with Web Crypto API support to function

faqs.question: Is Vaultwarden the same as Bitwarden?
faqs.answer: No, Vaultwarden is an unofficial, community-built alternative server implementation of the Bitwarden Client API, compatible with official Bitwarden clients.

faqs.question: Is Vaultwarden free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's free, open-source software licensed under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Was Vaultwarden previously called something else?
faqs.answer: Yes, it was formerly called Bitwarden_RS and was renamed in v1.21.0 to avoid trademark confusion.

faqs.question: What clients work with Vaultwarden?
faqs.answer: Official Bitwarden mobile, desktop, and browser clients.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted password management
use_cases.description: Individuals or organizations that want to run their own Bitwarden-compatible server instead of using Bitwarden's cloud service.

use_cases.title: Low-resource server deployments
use_cases.description: The Rust-based server is described as lightweight, suiting deployment on small home servers or low-cost VPS instances.
use_cases.audience: Home lab and small server operators

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Vaultwarden?
paragraphs: Vaultwarden is a free, open-source server, written in Rust, that implements the Bitwarden Client API so that official Bitwarden clients can connect to it.
It's a community project (formerly named Bitwarden_RS) rather than an official Bitwarden product, licensed under AGPL-3.0.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Vaultwarden is for
paragraphs: Vaultwarden is aimed at people who want to self-host a password manager compatible with official Bitwarden apps, rather than relying on Bitwarden's hosted cloud service.

enrichment.short_description: Self-hosted, open-source identity provider supporting OAuth2/OIDC, SAML, LDAP, SCIM, and RADIUS.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Authentik is an open-source, self-hosted identity provider with a free tier and Enterprise plans from $5/user/month.

features.title: Multi-protocol support
features.description: Supports OAuth2/OIDC, SAML2, SCIM, LDAP, RADIUS, and Kerberos.

features.title: Customizable policies
features.description: Fully customizable policies let teams automate authentication and access workflows.

features.title: MFA and passkeys
features.description: Supports multi-factor authentication along with WebAuthn/passkeys.

features.title: Conditional access
features.description: Includes GeoIP-based and impossible-travel detection for conditional access decisions.

features.title: Remote access proxy
features.description: Provides web-based RDP, VNC, and SSH access, plus a general application proxy.

features.title: Enterprise integrations
features.description: Integrates with 30+ services including Google Workspace, Microsoft Entra, Slack, GitHub Enterprise, Atlassian Cloud, AWS, Grafana, Stripe, and Salesforce.

pros: Free, open-source core with self-hosted deployment
Supports a wide range of identity protocols (OIDC, SAML, LDAP, SCIM, RADIUS)
Reports over 1 million installations
Integrates with 30+ enterprise services
Self-hosted architecture gives full control over identity infrastructure
cons: No official cloud-hosted option; self-hosting is required for every tier
Enterprise support tiers require paid subscriptions
Enterprise Plus tier starts at $20,000 annually

faqs.question: Is Authentik free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, the open-source edition is free and self-hosted.

faqs.question: Does Authentik offer cloud hosting?
faqs.answer: No, all tiers, including Enterprise, require self-hosting.

faqs.question: What protocols does Authentik support?
faqs.answer: OIDC, OAuth2, SAML2, SCIM, LDAP, RADIUS, Kerberos, and proxy-based access.

faqs.question: How much does Authentik Enterprise cost?
faqs.answer: $5 per internal user per month plus $0.02 per external user per month, billed annually.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted SSO for internal apps
use_cases.description: Organizations can run Authentik on Kubernetes, Docker Compose, or via Terraform to provide single sign-on to internal applications.

use_cases.title: B2B and B2C identity infrastructure
use_cases.description: Authentik supports both workforce and customer-facing identity use cases behind one deployment.

features: OIDC, SAML, LDAP, SCIM, RADIUS, Kerberos, Proxy
Google Workspace and Microsoft Entra ID integrations

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Authentik?
paragraphs: Authentik is a self-hosted, open-source identity provider that lets organizations run their own authentication and identity infrastructure rather than relying on a third-party cloud service.
It supports a broad set of identity protocols, including OIDC, SAML2, SCIM, LDAP, and RADIUS, and adds enterprise integrations and support on its paid tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Authentik is for
paragraphs: Authentik is aimed at organizations that want to self-host identity infrastructure for both workforce (B2B) and customer-facing (B2C) applications, with a free open-source edition and paid Enterprise tiers for additional integrations and support.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source identity infrastructure platform with hosted login, MFA, and multi-tenancy, free up to 100 daily active users.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ZITADEL is an open-source identity platform for authentication and authorization, free up to 100 daily active users.

features.title: Hosted login pages
features.description: Provides ready-made, hosted login pages for authentication.

features.title: MFA and passwordless
features.description: Supports multi-factor authentication and passwordless login via passkeys.

features.title: Social logins
features.description: Supports social login through Google, Microsoft, and GitHub.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Manage user permissions through role-based access control.

features.title: Multi-tenancy
features.description: Extend applications to new organizations for multi-tenant use cases.

features.title: gRPC and REST APIs
features.description: Modern gRPC and REST APIs support custom integrations.

features.title: ZITADEL Actions
features.description: Execute custom workflows after predefined authentication events.

pros: Free tier includes unlimited users and organizations, up to 100 daily active users
Open source with an active GitHub community (4,000+ stars reported)
ISO 27001 certified, SOC2 Type II certified, and OpenID certified
Data residency options across the EU, US, Switzerland, and Australia
Reports serving customers in 69 countries
cons: Free tier is capped at 100 daily active users
Pro plan caps out at 25,000 daily active users before requiring Enterprise
Enterprise pricing requires contacting sales

faqs.question: Is ZITADEL free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ZITADEL has a free plan covering unlimited users and organizations up to 100 daily active users.

faqs.question: How much does ZITADEL Pro cost?
faqs.answer: $100 per month, which includes up to 25,000 daily active users.

faqs.question: Where can ZITADEL data be hosted?
faqs.answer: ZITADEL offers data residency options in the EU, US, Switzerland, and Australia.

faqs.question: Is ZITADEL open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, ZITADEL is available as open source on GitHub.

use_cases.title: Startups needing free-tier authentication
use_cases.description: The free plan supports unlimited users and organizations up to 100 daily active users, suiting early-stage products.

use_cases.title: Multi-tenant SaaS platforms
use_cases.description: Organizations-based multi-tenancy lets SaaS companies manage separate customer tenants within one ZITADEL instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is ZITADEL?
paragraphs: ZITADEL is an identity infrastructure platform that provides authentication and authorization, describing itself as bridging open-source and enterprise identity solutions.
It offers a free tier for smaller projects, a $100/month Pro plan for growing usage, and custom Enterprise pricing for larger deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who ZITADEL is for
paragraphs: ZITADEL suits teams that want an open-source identity platform with a generous free tier, multi-tenant organization support, and compliance certifications like ISO 27001 and SOC2 Type II.

enrichment.short_description: Customer identity and access management platform with a free self-hosted Community Edition and cloud plans from $162/month.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: FusionAuth is a CIAM platform with a free Community Edition and cloud plans starting at $162/month, billed annually.

features.title: MFA and passkeys
features.description: Supports multi-factor authentication and passkey-based passwordless login.

features.title: Magic links
features.description: Passwordless login via magic links sent to users.

features.title: Single sign-on and social logins
features.description: Supports SSO across applications along with social login providers.

features.title: Role-based access control
features.description: Manage permissions across applications with role-based access control.

features.title: Machine-to-machine authentication
features.description: Supports authentication for services and machine clients, not just human users.

features.title: Webhooks and API-first architecture
features.description: Built API-first, with webhooks for integrating authentication events into other systems.

features.title: SDKs and quickstarts
features.description: Provides quickstarts and SDKs for languages and frameworks including .NET Core, Angular, Python, iOS, React, Java, Ruby, JavaScript, Vue, Flutter, Go, and PHP.

pros: Free Community Edition with core authentication features that can be self-hosted
Flexible deployment: self-hosted, cloud, on-premises, hybrid, or air-gapped
Broad SDK and quickstart support across many languages and frameworks
Usage-based cloud pricing intended to avoid surprise costs at scale, per the company
cons: Advanced features like breached password detection and application theming require paid plans
Essentials and Enterprise cloud plans start at $2,970/month
24/7 phone and email support is only available on the Enterprise plan

faqs.question: Is FusionAuth free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Community Edition is free to self-host with core authentication features.

faqs.question: What's the cheapest paid plan?
faqs.answer: The Starter cloud plan is $162 per month, billed annually.

faqs.question: Can FusionAuth be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, all plans support self-hosting via ZIP, DEB, RPM, Docker, or Kubernetes.

faqs.question: Does FusionAuth offer enterprise support?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Enterprise plan includes 24/7 phone and email support and a private Slack channel.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted authentication for developers
use_cases.description: Developers can run the free Community Edition on their own infrastructure with no user limit stated.

use_cases.title: Managed cloud CIAM for growing businesses
use_cases.description: Starter, Essentials, and Enterprise cloud plans add hosting, advanced MFA, and dedicated support as usage grows.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is FusionAuth?
paragraphs: FusionAuth is a customer identity and access management (CIAM) platform that handles authentication and user management for applications.
It offers a free, self-hosted Community Edition with core authentication features, plus paid FusionAuth Cloud plans that add hosting, advanced security, and support.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who FusionAuth is for
paragraphs: FusionAuth suits development teams that want to self-host authentication for free, or businesses that want a managed cloud CIAM platform with usage-based pricing as they scale.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source identity and access management platform supporting OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Keycloak is a free, open-source identity and access management platform supporting SSO, OIDC, OAuth 2.0, and SAML.

features.title: Single sign-on
features.description: Users authenticate with Keycloak rather than with each individual application.

features.title: Identity brokering and social login
features.description: Broker identities from external providers and support social login.

features.title: User federation
features.description: Connects to existing LDAP or Active Directory directories for user federation.

features.title: Admin console
features.description: Centralized admin console for managing realms, users, and clients.

features.title: Account management console
features.description: Self-service console lets users manage their own account details.

features.title: Fine-grained authorization
features.description: Authorization services go beyond simple role-based access control.

pros: Completely free and open source
A Cloud Native Computing Foundation (CNCF) incubating project
Supports major identity protocols: OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML
Federates with existing LDAP or Active Directory directories
Provides fine-grained authorization beyond simple role-based access
cons: No official managed cloud/hosted offering from the project itself
Self-hosting and configuration require operational expertise
No commercial vendor support included by default as a community project

faqs.question: Is Keycloak free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Keycloak is open-source software with no licensing cost.

faqs.question: What protocols does Keycloak support?
faqs.answer: OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML.

faqs.question: Can Keycloak connect to an existing directory?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports user federation with LDAP and Active Directory.

faqs.question: Who governs the Keycloak project?
faqs.answer: Keycloak is a Cloud Native Computing Foundation incubation project.

use_cases.title: Adding SSO to applications
use_cases.description: Organizations can deploy Keycloak to add single sign-on across multiple applications with minimal per-app effort.

use_cases.title: Federating identity with LDAP/Active Directory
use_cases.description: Enterprises with an existing directory can connect it to Keycloak for centralized identity management.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Keycloak?
paragraphs: Keycloak is an open-source identity and access management project that adds authentication and authorization to applications and services.
It's a Cloud Native Computing Foundation incubation project, distributed as server packages, containers, and a Kubernetes/OpenShift operator, with no licensing cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Keycloak is for
paragraphs: Keycloak is aimed at development and platform teams that want a free, self-hosted identity provider supporting OpenID Connect, OAuth 2.0, and SAML, with the ability to federate with LDAP or Active Directory.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source authentication platform, free under 5,000 monthly active users, with usage-based pricing for premium add-ons.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: SuperTokens is an open-source authentication platform, free under 5,000 MAU, with usage-based pricing for premium add-ons.

features.title: Email/password authentication
features.description: Standard email and password login flows.

features.title: Passwordless login
features.description: Supports passwordless login and magic links.

features.title: Social login
features.description: Login via third-party social providers.

features.title: Single sign-on
features.description: Supports SSO across applications.

features.title: Multi-tenancy
features.description: Supports multi-tenant and organization-based configurations.

features.title: Account linking
features.description: Links multiple login methods to a single user account.

features.title: Attack protection suite
features.description: A suite of protections against common authentication attacks.

features.title: Session management
features.description: Session management with configurable custom policies.

pros: Core features are free at any scale when self-hosted
Cloud managed service is free for under 5,000 monthly active users
The company states it works with 25+ frameworks and languages, including React, Angular, Vue, Python, Go, Android, Flutter, and iOS
SOC2 compliance mentioned for the managed dashboard
Recognized in the ROSS Index as a fastest-growing open-source startup in 2022
cons: Premium add-ons (MFA, account linking) are billed per monthly active user with a $100/month minimum
Multi-tenancy, machine-to-machine auth, and the attack protection suite require contacting sales
Additional dashboard users beyond the first three cost $20/user/month

faqs.question: Is SuperTokens free?
faqs.answer: Yes, core authentication features are free at any scale when self-hosted, and the managed cloud service is free under 5,000 monthly active users.

faqs.question: How is SuperTokens priced above the free tier?
faqs.answer: Premium add-ons like multi-factor authentication and account linking are billed per monthly active user, with a $100/month minimum.

faqs.question: What frameworks does SuperTokens support?
faqs.answer: The company states it works with 25+ frameworks including React, Angular, Vue, Python, Go, Android, Flutter, and iOS.

faqs.question: Is SuperTokens open source?
faqs.answer: Yes, it's an open-source authentication platform with a self-hosted option.

use_cases.title: Developers building custom login flows
use_cases.description: Teams that want an open-source, self-hostable authentication system they can customize.

use_cases.title: Startups needing cost-effective authentication
use_cases.description: The free cloud tier under 5,000 monthly active users suits early-stage products.

features: All core features free at any scale
Pay only for premium add-ons if used

long_form_blocks.heading: What is SuperTokens?
paragraphs: SuperTokens is an open-source authentication platform that lets developers build and customize login systems, positioning itself as a lower-cost alternative to services like Auth0.
Core features are free at any scale when self-hosted, and the managed cloud service is free for up to 5,000 monthly active users, with premium add-ons billed per user beyond that.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who SuperTokens is for
paragraphs: SuperTokens is aimed at development teams that want an open-source, customizable authentication system, whether self-hosted for free or used via the managed cloud service.

enrichment.short_description: Open-source identity and access management platform for customer, B2B, and machine identity, founded in 2019.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Ory is an open-source identity and access management platform with a free Developer tier and plans from $770/year.

features.title: Customer identity (CIAM)
features.description: Handles login and signup for customer-facing applications.

features.title: B2B identity
features.description: Enterprise SSO, SAML, and SCIM support with granular permissions for B2B use cases.

features.title: Machine-to-machine authentication
features.description: Supports identity and access management for services and automated agents, not just human users.

features.title: Stateless horizontal scaling
features.description: Architecture designed for stateless horizontal scaling described by the company as supporting very large deployments.

features.title: Observability
features.description: Provides metrics and trace-level logging for production observability.

features.title: Multiple deployment models
features.description: Available as open-source self-hosted software, an Ory Enterprise License for private infrastructure, or the fully managed Ory Network.

pros: Free Developer tier includes 2 development environments and community support
Open source, with 48,000+ GitHub stars reported by the company
Multiple deployment options: self-hosted, enterprise license, or fully managed
Reports managing 3.3 billion identities across its network
Data residency and multi-region deployment available on higher tiers
cons: Growth plan starts at $9,350/year
B2B SSO on the Growth plan is limited to OIDC only
Enterprise and self-hosted Enterprise License pricing require contacting sales

faqs.question: When was Ory founded?
faqs.answer: Ory was founded on June 21, 2019.

faqs.question: Is Ory free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ory offers a free Developer tier with 2 development environments and community support.

faqs.question: How much does Ory's Production plan cost?
faqs.answer: $770 per year.

faqs.question: Can Ory be self-hosted?
faqs.answer: Yes, Ory offers an open-source self-hosted option as well as an Ory Enterprise License for on-premises or private cloud deployment.

use_cases.title: Startups needing a free identity tier
use_cases.description: The free Developer tier gives smaller projects access to core identity features with community support.

use_cases.title: Enterprises needing B2B SSO and multi-tenant identity
use_cases.description: Growth and Enterprise plans add B2B SSO, multiple organizations, and higher uptime SLAs for larger companies.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Ory?
paragraphs: Ory is an identity and access management platform, founded in 2019, that covers customer identity (CIAM), B2B identity with enterprise SSO and SCIM, and identity for automated agents and services.

It's available as open-source self-hosted software, through an Ory Enterprise License for private infrastructure, or as the fully managed Ory Network, with a free Developer tier and paid plans starting at $770/year.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Ory is for
paragraphs: Ory suits companies that need identity infrastructure spanning customer logins, B2B enterprise SSO, and machine/service authentication, with deployment options ranging from a free open-source self-hosted setup to a fully managed enterprise network.
